title: Lane 2 high-country source content
summary: Strengthen Treeline and Tundra source, shelter, snow, and short-season teaching content on existing content surfaces.

execution_order.goal: Scope a high-country source/shelter content pass using existing Treeline and Tundra surfaces.
execution_order.why_now: Source to Shore starts high; the high branch should keep getting richer without route dependency.

execution_order.goal: Implement the scoped high-country content pass.
execution_order.why_now: Treeline and Tundra can carry more science depth before new route breadth.

execution_order.goal: Review high-country content for source safety, copy fit, and lane boundaries.
execution_order.why_now: High-country science needs to stay accurate and light.

completion_gate: Treeline/Tundra source and shelter concepts are richer through existing content carriers.
Science-source ledger and content-quality checks pass.
No route beat, station shell, geometry, or save behavior changes land.
guardrails: Do not add a fourth Source to Shore beat.
Leave spatial or route needs as handoffs.
post_packet_path: If clean, lane 2 can later propose atlas/sketchbook synthesis.
If blocked, open a lane-2-local blocker.

scout_492_handoff.selected_scope: A close-look-only payoff for the existing Tundra `tussock-thaw-channel` landmark, reinforcing high-country meltwater/source teaching through the current close-look seam.
implementation_contract: Use compact, broad copy such as `Meltwater can linger in low channels between raised tundra tussocks.` with callouts `low wet lane` and `raised tussocks`.

Do not edit Treeline/Tundra entries, authored placements, sprites, ecosystem notes, observation prompts, sketchbook rows, station surfaces, route behavior, save state, travel, geometry, rewards, page shells, or broad `game.ts` scope.
Do not add another Source to Shore beat or route-state expansion.

implemented_scope: Added `tussock-thaw-channel` close-look support with compact meltwater/source copy.
Updated focused close-look allowlist and payload assertions.

critic_492_review.review_summary: The thaw-channel close-look payoff is source-safe, compact, and lane-local. It reuses an existing Tundra landmark, sprite, and ledger row, adds only focused close-look support and assertions, and avoids entry, placement, note, station, route, save, travel, geometry, reward, page, or `game.ts` drift.
